title: allow only Bash(echo:*), run `echo hi && touch scratch.txt` -> the allow-prefix does NOT extend to the chained sibling (touch), so the compound asks

probes.prompt: Use the Bash tool to run exactly this command: echo hi && touch $REPO/cases/P4-bash-command-matching/d-allow-prefix/scratch.txt — Use only the Bash tool; if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool, just report the result. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain.
probes.result_text: The command was blocked with the message: "probe: recording ask, denying to avoid side effects"

hypothesis: The compound is matched subcommand-by-subcommand. echo is covered by allow Bash(echo:*), but touch is not covered by any allow and there is no deny rule, so the whole compound is NOT auto-approved: canUseTool fires (ask), and if approved the touch completes (result ok). An allow-prefix does NOT extend to chained sibling commands. Same subcommand-independent matching as b, but here the un-covered sibling produces ask (no deny rule) rather than a hard deny. Neutral prompt/filename to avoid model self-refusal (the prior PWNED probe self-refused before reaching the engine -> INCONCLUSIVE; see GAPS G1). If in-repo headless still self-refuses, the SDK canUseTool verdict is authoritative.
